1.Name: PG17 · source · default PGDATA (localhost:5432)
1.Comment: PostgreSQL 17 — default PGDATA (/var/lib/postgresql/data). ecommerce_db, analytics_db, inventory_db + ~50 MB sample data.

2.Name: PG18 · source · custom PGDATA (localhost:5434)
2.Comment: PostgreSQL 18 — custom PGDATA (/var/lib/postgresql/18/main). Same sample data as PG17 source.

3.Name: PG17 · WAL archiving source (localhost:5436)
3.Comment: PostgreSQL 17 — archive_mode=on. WAL segments archived to Storj via cloud-dump wal-push. Use this instance for WAL archiving and PITR testing.

4.Name: PG17 · restore-target (localhost:5433)
4.Comment: Restore target for PG17 backups. Fresh: test_user + restore_marker. After restore: source data replaces everything.

5.Name: PG18 · restore-target · custom PGDATA (localhost:5435)
5.Comment: Restore target for PG18 backups (custom PGDATA). Fresh: test_user + restore_marker. After restore: source data replaces everything.

6.Name: PG17 · WAL restore-target (localhost:5438)
6.Comment: PITR restore target. Populated by cloud-dump restore with --recovery-target-time. PostgreSQL replays archived WAL from Storj then promotes. Start with: make wal-restore-start
